title: EeroQ Validates CMOS-Controlled Electron Shuttling on Superfluid Helium for All-to-All Qubit Routing - Quantum Computing Report
gist: EeroQ published peer-reviewed results showing its CMOS-controlled electron-shuttling architecture on superfluid helium achieves zero measurable charge loss over a billion transport cycles, validating a wiring-efficient, all-to-all qubit connectivity design built on standard commercial chip fabrication.

insights.claim: EeroQ's 'Wonder Lake' chip organizes electron-packet transport as a macro-scale Charge-Coupled Device (CCD), a multiplexing layout borrowed from digital-imaging silicon sensor grids, to solve the wiring bottleneck that plagues scaling trapped-electron and quantum-dot qubit platforms.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Reusing proven CCD multiplexing logic rather than a novel wiring scheme could de-risk EeroQ's path to manufacturable, scaled qubit arrays.
insights.quote: EeroQ addresses this scaling barrier by organizing its “Wonder Lake” prototype chip as a macro-scale Charge-Coupled Device (CCD), a multiplexing layout similar to the silicon sensor grids used in digital imaging hardware.
insights.locator: The Architecture of Superfluid Charge-Coupled Devices

insights.claim: As qubit counts increase, conventional trapped-electron or quantum-dot architectures typically require a dedicated, non-local control line for every trapping site, causing heat-routing and geometric congestion inside the dilution refrigerator stack — the core scaling barrier EeroQ's design is meant to solve.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Names a specific engineering bottleneck facing rival solid-state qubit architectures, useful for comparative technical diligence.
insights.quote: conventional architectures typically require a dedicated, non-local control line for every single trapping site, leading to massive heat routing and geometric congestion over the dilution refrigerator stack
insights.locator: The Architecture of Superfluid Charge-Coupled Devices

insights.claim: The chip uses just 14 external control lines to drive 128 independently addressable channels, with a repeating 'unit cell' layout replicated 32 times across the monolithic array.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A 14-line-to-128-channel multiplexing ratio is a concrete, comparable wiring-efficiency metric against competing qubit modalities.
insights.quote: Using just 14 global control lines, the on-chip CMOS circuitry selectively guides electron packets containing tens of particles down to isolated, single-electron qubits through any of 128 parallel transport pathways.
insights.locator: The Architecture of Superfluid Charge-Coupled Devices

insights.claim: Liquid helium lacks the chemical dangling bonds and lattice defects found in solid semiconductors, forming a uniform potential surface for floating electrons and thereby avoiding the material impurities and charge traps that typically cause phase decoherence and signal degradation in solid-state quantum architectures.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Positions helium-based electron qubits as a materials-science route around a persistent solid-state qubit coherence problem, relevant when comparing qubit modalities.
insights.quote: bypassing the material impurities and charge traps that typically cause phase decoherence and signal degradation in solid-state quantum architectures

insights.claim: In peer-reviewed testing, the electron packets completed at least 10^9 continuous transfer cycles with zero detectable charge loss, and in aggregate traveled tens of kilometers across the microchannel layout while remaining stably floating above the substrate.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: A billion-cycle, zero-loss transport result is a strong, quantifiable reliability benchmark investors can compare against other shuttling-qubit or trapped-ion transport claims.
insights.quote: The electron packets successfully completed at least 109 continuous transfer cycles without a single verifiable charge dropping from the potential wells.
insights.locator: Lossless Kilometric Transport and All-to-All Scaling

insights.claim: The device was fabricated at commercial foundry SkyWater Technology using a standard 130-nanometer CMOS process, meaning EeroQ can scale using existing commercial semiconductor manufacturing lines rather than building bespoke cleanroom fabrication tools.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Reliance on an existing commercial 130nm node rather than a bespoke process is a material cost and scaling advantage that lowers manufacturing risk versus competitors needing custom fabs.
insights.quote: allowing EeroQ to leverage existing commercial semiconductor manufacturing lines to scale its hardware without building bespoke cleanroom fabrication tools
insights.locator: Lossless Kilometric Transport and All-to-All Scaling

insights.claim: The research, supervised by lead author K.E. Castoria and CEO Nick Farina, was published in Physical Review Applied under the title 'Selective shuttling of electrons on helium using a CMOS control platform.'
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: Peer review in a respected physics journal adds external validation credibility to EeroQ's technical claims.
insights.quote: Published in Physical Review Applied (“Selective shuttling of electrons on helium using a CMOS control platform“)

insights.claim: The routing efficiency enables all-to-all qubit connectivity, letting distant qubits be mechanically shuttled next to each other for fast two-qubit entangling gates, which simplifies layout requirements for high-distance topological error correction.
insights.speaker: Mohamed Abdel-Kareem
insights.so_what: All-to-all connectivity is a structural advantage for implementing high-distance error-correcting codes, a key requirement for fault-tolerant quantum computing at scale.
insights.quote: This capability to dynamically shuffle information across a standard microelectronics footprint simplifies the layout requirements for high-distance topological error correction
insights.locator: Lossless Kilometric Transport and All-to-All Scaling
